title: Why I Am Not Going to Buy a Computer
excerpt: Reflecting on Wendell Berry's critique of technology and the importance of human effort in the age of AI
content_html:
2026-07-20

#philosophy  #ai 

The text on the computer screen, and the computer printout too, has a sterile, untouched, factory made look, like that of a plastic whistle or a new car. The body does not do work like that. The body characterizes everything it touches. What it makes it traces over with the marks of its pulses and breathings, its excitements, hesitations, flaws, and mistakes. On its good work, it leaves the marks of skill, care, and love persisting through hesitations, flaws, and mistakes. And to those of us who love and honor the life of the body in this world, these marks are precious things, necessities of life.

— Wendell Berry

I have yet to read a more prescient quote than this one by Wendell Berry on his writing Why I am Not Going to Buy a Computer. Berry wrote on continuing to write by hand and by typewriters, and his refusal to buy a computer in the late 1980s. However, this exact emotion in his writing can be felt, even more so today, almost 40 years later. As we become more disconnected from the work we do, let writings and art be created by AI, we forgo the meaning, emotions, and learning behind such work. Anything done autonomously is not done by someone at all. A human has nothing to inherently gain from it. The creation process is completely stripped out. And I believe this lack of connection to the work and what this quote represents mimics the exacts reason why there is so much passion against the creation of media, writings, art, videos, etc. created by AI. There is no remnant of thought put into such a piece. It feels devoid of life, yet still full of flaws and mistakes, the exact opposite of something that has been worked on with care. The “body” has not put a mark on the piece. Technology, at least from Berry’s perspective, at the time at least meant a sort of perfection without such mistakes. Those that can be erased by a backspace, not the mess shown when crossing out and erasing words by pen and paper. Nonetheless, AI does not yet even have this quality of “perfection”, and it being marketed otherwise, as something even sparkling and magical, making it seem like the average person shouldn’t even be able to comprehend such a technology, that they should be in awe of it1, is what makes it even more egregious to have such flaws in it output and even more outrageous to those most affected in creative spaces.

As just a software engineer, I am not so ignorant and appalling to even closely compare software or coding to art. Quite the opposite, AI as a means to an ends for coding is mostly a positive. It truly is just much faster than writing, and at a corporate level, outside of system design, there isn’t necessarily meant to be an art or a craft to the boilerplate code on something already well defined (a personal and passionate side project is not in scope of this comment). While this only proves that coding was never the bottleneck, it still exemplifies and further proves the amount of human work and care that needs to go into a product even in large industries. AI is probabilistic and any probabilistic system can inherently never be perfect as it also still makes numerous mistakes. Work still needs to be reviewed by humans. AI is not so elegant, to make a comparison similar to Berry, as the humble, sterile Microsoft Word.

All this to say that, if anything, it has only become more obvious how much human work is needed in society when people are laid off, attempted to be replaced, and finding even Anthropic still having job opening for Software Engineers. As Berry closed out his work, I will as well, as again it still shows how timeless feelings across various technological advances, people remain consistent. “… I am not without hope. I knew a man who, in the age of chainsaws, went right on cutting his wood with a handsaw and an axe. He was a healthier and a saner man than I am. I shall let his memory trouble my thoughts.”

References#

  • Berry, Wendell. “Why I am not going to buy a computer.” (1987).

  1. and in awe of it they are, as such marketing targets those with lesser understanding, as made prevalent by the paper: Lower Artificial Intelligence Literacy Predicts Greater AI Receptivity. https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/t9u8g_v1 ↩︎

blocks.html_en:
The text on the computer screen, and the computer printout too, has a sterile, untouched, factory made look, like that of a plastic whistle or a new car. The body does not do work like that. The body characterizes everything it touches. What it makes it traces over with the marks of its pulses and breathings, its excitements, hesitations, flaws, and mistakes. sentences.en: The text on the computer screen, and the computer printout too, has a sterile, untouched, factory made look, like that of a plastic whistle or a new car.
sentences.zh: 计算机屏幕上的文字以及计算机打印输出都具有一种无菌的、未经修改的、工厂制造的外观，就像塑料哨子或新车的外观。

sentences.en: The body does not do work like that.
sentences.zh: 身体不会那样做工作。

sentences.en: The body characterizes everything it touches.
sentences.zh: 身体表征了它所接触到的一切。

sentences.en: What it makes it traces over with the marks of its pulses and breathings, its excitements, hesitations, flaws, and mistakes.
sentences.zh: 它所做的一切都用它的脉搏和呼吸的痕迹、它的兴奋、犹豫、缺陷和错误来描绘。

sentences.en: On its good work, it leaves the marks of skill, care, and love persisting through hesitations, flaws, and mistakes.
sentences.zh: 在其出色的工作中，它留下了技巧、关怀和爱的痕迹，尽管犹豫、缺陷和错误仍然存​​在。

sentences.en: And to those of us who love and honor the life of the body in this world, these marks are precious things, necessities of life. — Wendell Berry
sentences.zh: 对于我们这些在这个世界上热爱和尊重身体生命的人来说，这些标记是宝贵的东西，是生活的必需品。 — 温德尔·贝里

sentences.en: I have yet to read a more prescient quote than this one by Wendell Berry on his writing Why I am Not Going to Buy a Computer.
sentences.zh: 我还没有读过比温德尔·贝里 (Wendell Berry) 在他的著作《为什么我不打算买电脑》中的这句话更有先见之明的言论。

sentences.en: Berry wrote on continuing to write by hand and by typewriters, and his refusal to buy a computer in the late 1980s.
sentences.zh: 贝里写道，他继续用手和打字机写作，并在 20 世纪 80 年代末拒绝购买电脑。

sentences.en: However, this exact emotion in his writing can be felt, even more so today, almost 40 years later.
sentences.zh: 然而，这种情感在他的笔下依然清晰可见，在近四十年后的今天更是如此。

sentences.en: As we become more disconnected from the work we do, let writings and art be created by AI, we forgo the meaning, emotions, and learning behind such work.
sentences.zh: 当我们与自己所做的工作越来越脱节，让人工智能创作文字和艺术时，我们就放弃了这些工作背后的意义、情感和学习。

sentences.en: Anything done autonomously is not done by someone at all.
sentences.zh: 任何自主完成的事情根本就不是由某人完成的。

sentences.en: A human has nothing to inherently gain from it.
sentences.zh: 人类本身并没有从中获得任何好处。

sentences.en: The creation process is completely stripped out.
sentences.zh: 创建过程被完全剥离。

sentences.en: And I believe this lack of connection to the work and what this quote represents mimics the exacts reason why there is so much passion against the creation of media, writings, art, videos, etc. created by AI.
sentences.zh: 我相信，这种与作品缺乏联系以及这句话所代表的内容，正是人们对人工智能创造的媒体、文字、艺术、视频等充满热情的确切原因。

sentences.en: There is no remnant of thought put into such a piece.
sentences.zh: 这样的作品中没有任何思想残留。

sentences.en: It feels devoid of life, yet still full of flaws and mistakes, the exact opposite of something that has been worked on with care.
sentences.zh: 它感觉缺乏生气，但仍然充满缺陷和错误，与精心制作的东西完全相反。

sentences.en: The “body” has not put a mark on the piece.
sentences.zh: “身体”并没有在作品上留下痕迹。

sentences.en: Technology, at least from Berry’s perspective, at the time at least meant a sort of perfection without such mistakes.
sentences.zh: 技术，至少从贝瑞的角度来看，在当时至少意味着一种完美，没有这样的错误。

sentences.en: Those that can be erased by a backspace, not the mess shown when crossing out and erasing words by pen and paper.
sentences.zh: 那些可以通过退格键擦除的内容，而不是用笔和纸划掉和擦除单词时显示的混乱。

sentences.en: Nonetheless, AI does not yet even have this quality of “perfection”, and it being marketed otherwise, as something even sparkling and magical, making it seem like the average person shouldn’t even be able to comprehend such a technology, that they should be in awe of it1, is what makes it even more egregious to have such flaws in it output and even more outrageous to those most affected in creative spaces.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，人工智能甚至还没有达到这种“完美”的品质，而它的营销方式却是闪闪发光和神奇的，让普通人看起来甚至不应该能够理解这样的技术，他们应该对它感到敬畏，这使得它的输出中存在这样的缺陷更加令人震惊，对于那些在创意领域受影响最严重的人来说更加令人愤慨。

blocks.html_en: As just a software engineer, I am not so ignorant and appalling to even closely compare software or coding to art. Quite the opposite, AI as a means to an ends for coding is mostly a positive. It truly is just much faster than writing, and at a corporate level, outside of system design, there isn’t necessarily meant to be an art or a craft to the boilerplate code on something already well defined (a personal and passionate side project is not in scope of this comment). While this only proves that coding was never the bottleneck, it still exemplifies and further proves the amount of human work and care that needs to go into a product even in large industries. AI is probabilistic and any probabilistic system can inherently never be perfect as it also still makes numerous mistakes. Work still needs to be reviewed by humans. AI is not so elegant, to make a comparison similar to Berry, as the humble, sterile Microsoft Word.

sentences.en: As just a software engineer, I am not so ignorant and appalling to even closely compare software or coding to art.
sentences.zh: 作为一名软件工程师，我并没有那么无知和可怕，甚至无法将软件或编码与艺术进行密切比较。

sentences.en: Quite the opposite, AI as a means to an ends for coding is mostly a positive.
sentences.zh: 恰恰相反，人工智能作为实现编码目的的手段基本上是积极的。

sentences.en: It truly is just much faster than writing, and at a corporate level, outside of system design, there isn’t necessarily meant to be an art or a craft to the boilerplate code on something already well defined (a personal and passionate side project is not in scope of this comment).
sentences.zh: 它确实比编写快得多，并且在公司层面，在系统设计之外，在已经明确定义的东西上的样板代码不一定意味着是一门艺术或工艺（个人和热情的业余项目不在本评论的范围内）。

sentences.en: While this only proves that coding was never the bottleneck, it still exemplifies and further proves the amount of human work and care that needs to go into a product even in large industries.
sentences.zh: 虽然这只能证明编码从来都不是瓶颈，但它仍然例证并进一步证明了即使在大型行业中，产品也需要投入大量的人力和精力。

sentences.en: AI is probabilistic and any probabilistic system can inherently never be perfect as it also still makes numerous mistakes.
sentences.zh: 人工智能是概率性的，任何概率系统本质上都不可能是完美的，因为它仍然会犯很多错误。

sentences.en: Work still needs to be reviewed by humans.
sentences.zh: 工作仍然需要由人类来审查。

sentences.en: AI is not so elegant, to make a comparison similar to Berry, as the humble, sterile Microsoft Word.
sentences.zh: 人工智能并不那么优雅，就像 Berry 一样，就像卑微、枯燥的 Microsoft Word 一样。

blocks.html_en: All this to say that, if anything, it has only become more obvious how much human work is needed in society when people are laid off, attempted to be replaced, and finding even Anthropic still having job opening for Software Engineers. As Berry closed out his work, I will as well, as again it still shows how timeless feelings across various technological advances, people remain consistent. “… I am not without hope. I knew a man who, in the age of chainsaws, went right on cutting his wood with a handsaw and an axe. He was a healthier and a saner man than I am. I shall let his memory trouble my thoughts.”

sentences.en: All this to say that, if anything, it has only become more obvious how much human work is needed in society when people are laid off, attempted to be replaced, and finding even Anthropic still having job opening for Software Engineers.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都表明，当人们被解雇、试图被取代，甚至发现 Anthropic 仍然有软件工程师的职位空缺时，社会需要多少人力工作才会变得更加明显。

sentences.en: As Berry closed out his work, I will as well, as again it still shows how timeless feelings across various technological advances, people remain consistent.
sentences.zh: 当贝里结束他的工作时，我也会这样做，因为它仍然表明人们在各种技术进步中如何保持永恒的感觉始终如一。

sentences.en: I am not without hope.
sentences.zh: 我并非没有希望。

sentences.en: I knew a man who, in the age of chainsaws, went right on cutting his wood with a handsaw and an axe.
sentences.zh: 我认识一个人，在电锯时代，他一直用手锯和斧头锯木头。

sentences.en: He was a healthier and a saner man than I am.
sentences.zh: 他比我更健康、更理智。

sentences.en: I shall let his memory trouble my thoughts.”
sentences.zh: 我会让他的记忆困扰我的思绪。”

sentences.en: References#
sentences.zh: 参考＃

sentences.en: and in awe of it they are, as such marketing targets those with lesser understanding, as made prevalent by the paper: Lower Artificial Intelligence Literacy Predicts Greater AI Receptivity.
sentences.zh: 他们对此感到敬畏，因为这种营销针对的是那些了解较少的人，正如论文中所流行的那样：较低的人工智能素养预示着更高的人工智能接受度。

content_html_zh:
2026-07-20

#philosophy  #ai 
计算机屏幕上的文字以及计算机打印输出都具有一种无菌的、未经修改的、工厂制造的外观，就像塑料哨子或新车的外观。 身体不会那样做工作。 身体表征了它所接触到的一切。 它所做的一切都用它的脉搏和呼吸的痕迹、它的兴奋、犹豫、缺陷和错误来描绘。 在其出色的工作中，它留下了技巧、关怀和爱的痕迹，尽管犹豫、缺陷和错误仍然存​​在。 对于我们这些在这个世界上热爱和尊重身体生命的人来说，这些标记是宝贵的东西，是生活的必需品。 — 温德尔·贝里

我还没有读过比温德尔·贝里 (Wendell Berry) 在他的著作《为什么我不打算买电脑》中的这句话更有先见之明的言论。 贝里写道，他继续用手和打字机写作，并在 20 世纪 80 年代末拒绝购买电脑。 然而，这种情感在他的笔下依然清晰可见，在近四十年后的今天更是如此。 当我们与自己所做的工作越来越脱节，让人工智能创作文字和艺术时，我们就放弃了这些工作背后的意义、情感和学习。 任何自主完成的事情根本就不是由某人完成的。 人类本身并没有从中获得任何好处。 创建过程被完全剥离。 我相信，这种与作品缺乏联系以及这句话所代表的内容，正是人们对人工智能创造的媒体、文字、艺术、视频等充满热情的确切原因。 这样的作品中没有任何思想残留。 它感觉缺乏生气，但仍然充满缺陷和错误，与精心制作的东西完全相反。 “身体”并没有在作品上留下痕迹。 技术，至少从贝瑞的角度来看，在当时至少意味着一种完美，没有这样的错误。 那些可以通过退格键擦除的内容，而不是用笔和纸划掉和擦除单词时显示的混乱。 尽管如此，人工智能甚至还没有达到这种“完美”的品质，而它的营销方式却是闪闪发光和神奇的，让普通人看起来甚至不应该能够理解这样的技术，他们应该对它感到敬畏，这使得它的输出中存在这样的缺陷更加令人震惊，对于那些在创意领域受影响最严重的人来说更加令人愤慨。

作为一名软件工程师，我并没有那么无知和可怕，甚至无法将软件或编码与艺术进行密切比较。 恰恰相反，人工智能作为实现编码目的的手段基本上是积极的。 它确实比编写快得多，并且在公司层面，在系统设计之外，在已经明确定义的东西上的样板代码不一定意味着是一门艺术或工艺（个人和热情的业余项目不在本评论的范围内）。 虽然这只能证明编码从来都不是瓶颈，但它仍然例证并进一步证明了即使在大型行业中，产品也需要投入大量的人力和精力。 人工智能是概率性的，任何概率系统本质上都不可能是完美的，因为它仍然会犯很多错误。 工作仍然需要由人类来审查。 人工智能并不那么优雅，就像 Berry 一样，就像卑微、枯燥的 Microsoft Word 一样。

所有这些都表明，当人们被解雇、试图被取代，甚至发现 Anthropic 仍然有软件工程师的职位空缺时，社会需要多少人力工作才会变得更加明显。 当贝里结束他的工作时，我也会这样做，因为它仍然表明人们在各种技术进步中如何保持永恒的感觉始终如一。 “… 我并非没有希望。 我认识一个人，在电锯时代，他一直用手锯和斧头锯木头。 他比我更健康、更理智。 我会让他的记忆困扰我的思绪。”

参考＃

  • 贝里、温德尔。 “为什么我不打算买电脑。” (1987).
  1. 他们对此感到敬畏，因为这种营销针对的是那些了解较少的人，正如论文中所流行的那样：较低的人工智能素养预示着更高的人工智能接受度。 https://osf.io/preprints/psyarxiv/t9u8g_v1 ↩︎
